data.title: New Zealand Shares Rise Despite Tariff-Induced Fall in Global Indexes, Scales Ups Stake in Unit
data.description:
New Zealand shares Rose on Tuesday despite most global indexes seeing a decline after increasing US-China trade war tensions.

The S&P/NZX 50 Index rose 0.47% or 56.58 points to close at 12,067.92.

Trump administration has started probes into pharmaceutical and chip imports as part of its developing tariff plans on the products, Reuters reported.

Reserve Bank of New Zealand's operating budget will be reduced by roughly 25% in the coming fiscal year and set at roughly NZ$150 million for each of the next five years, New Zealand Finance Minister Nicola Willis said on Wednesday.

"The new agreement will ensure that the Reserve Bank has adequate resources to fulfill its legal responsibilities while promoting heightened cost efficiency," Willis said.

In the local economy, The Reserve Bank of New Zealand launched Kiwi-GDP, a "nowcasting" model providing live estimates for gross domestic product in each quarter based on frequently updated data releases.

The latest Kiwi-GDP data showed a declining GDP in the last four weeks till April 11, with the nowcast dropping to 0.7699% from 1.0009% on March 21.

In corporate news, Scales acquired a further 7.5% holding in its unit Shelby Joint Venture with Brett Frankel for $24.4 million.

EBOS Group opened a non-underwritten retail offer on Wednesday, aiming to raise up to AU$50 million, or NZ$54 million.

data.title: AlphaValue/Baader Europe Downgrades H&M to Add, Cuts EPS Forecasts
data.description:
AlphaValue/Baader Europe lowered its earnings projections for H & M Hennes & Mauritz (HM-B.ST), citing the Swedish fashion retailer's slow revenue growth amid weakening demand.

The research firm also downgraded the company to add from buy, with a price target of 150 kronor.

Analysts now forecast adjusted EPS at 6.07 kronor in 2025 and 6.84 kronor in 2026, down from previous estimates of 8.58 kronor and 9.30 kronor, respectively.

"Profitability remains under pressure due to external headwinds, elevated markdowns and sustained investment in the customer offerings. Despite trading at a discount to its peers, H&M continues to face challenges with regaining momentum. The management has yet to quantify progress or provide a clear timeline on the mid-term targets," AlphaValue/Baader Europe wrote.

The research firm added that H&M remains "vulnerable" to global trade volatility, with the US being its second-largest market and much of its product sourcing tied to Asia.

data.title: Oil India Secures All Nine Blocks in Ninth Round of Open Acreage Licensing Policy
data.description:
Oil India secured all nine blocks it bid for under the ninth round of the Open Acreage Licensing Policy, adding over 51,000 square kilometers to its exploration portfolio, according to a Tuesday filing with the Indian bourses.

The company will operate six blocks independently and three in partnership, increasing its total exploration acreage to 110,000 square kilometers from 60,000 square kilometers, the filing said.

data.title: DEWA to Halt Trading in Dubai
data.description:

The electricity and water services company will resume trading after the meeting results are disclosed, according to a same-day release.

data.title: Soybeans slip on tariff woes, ample South American supply
data.description:
Chicago soybean futures fell for a third session on Wednesday, slipping further from Monday's seven-week high amid strong supply from South America and as tariffs strangle Chinese demand for U.S. beans.

Corn and wheat futures also fell despite a renewed weakening of the U.S. dollar making U.S. exports more competitive.

The most-active soybean contract on the Chicago Board of Trade (CBOT) was down 0.5% at $10.31 a bushel at 0518 GMT after moving as high as $10.49-1/2 on Monday.

CBOT July corn fell 0.3% to $4.88 a bushel and July wheat was down 0.5% at $5.53 a bushel.

The dollar index was down 0.5%, reversing some of Tuesday's gains and moving back towards three-year lows reached after U.S. President Donald Trump unleashed his tariff policies.

China, by far the biggest buyer of U.S. soybeans and a smaller buyer of U.S. corn and wheat, has imposed counter-tariffs on the United States that effectively prohibit crop imports.

"The U.S. will have to make a deal with China or it will end up having to store a lot of unsold soybeans," said Ole Houe, director of advisory services at IKON Commodities in Sydney.

A big soybean surplus would fill U.S. storage facilities and leave little space during harvest later this year for other crops like corn, which would have to be sold more quickly, likely at lower prices, Houe said.

Meanwhile, the U.S. national soybean crush in March fell below most trade estimates, the average daily processing pace declining for a third straight month, industry data showed.

Overseas, Brazil is wrapping up a massive soybean harvest, and traders think efforts by the Argentinian government to stimulate exports could result in larger soy shipments from the country.

In other crops, wheat is under pressure from weather forecasts predicting much-needed rain in the coming days in the U.S. Plains.

Argentina's 2025/26 wheat harvest is set to grow by 10.2% to 20.5 million metric tons this year, the head of economic studies at the Buenos Aires grains exchange said.

India is likely to see above-average monsoon rains for the second straight year in 2025, the government said, raising expectations of higher farm output.

data.title: Interim Report Byggmax Group 1 January - 31 March, 2025
data.description:
Byggmax

Increased sales resulted in improved profitability

"Increased sales and strong margin contributed to improved earnings in the first quarter of 2025. Our determined work has ensured stability that enables full focus on what lies ahead. With well-stocked inventories, improved stores and product launches, Byggmax is ready for a new high season".

Karl Sandlund, President and CEO

The first quarter 2025

  • Net sales amounted to SEK 929 M (866), representing an increase of 7.2 percent.
  • Exchange rate effects had a negative impact on net sales of 0.7 percent. The Group’s like-for-like sales increased by 7.6 percent.
  • The comparable costs, i.e. costs excluding new and closed stores, increased SEK 4 M (-32).
  • EBITA amounted to SEK -109 M (-148), with an EBITA margin of -11.7 percent (-17.1).

This is information that Byggmax Group AB (publ) is obliged to make public pursuant to the EU Market Abuse Regulation. The information was submitted for publication on April 16, 2025 at 07:45 am CET.

For further information, please contact:

Helena Nathhorst, CFO

Mobile: +46 76 119 00 40

E-mail: helena.nathhorst@byggmax.se

Karl Sandlund, President and CEO

Mobile: +46 76 119 01 84

E-mail: karl.sandlund@byggmax.se

About Byggmax Group

Byggmax is a leading Nordic retail chain specializing in building materials and DIY products, with more than 210 stores across Sweden, Norway, Finland, and Denmark. Our business model is simple - we offer high-quality products for DIYers at the best prices on the market. Shopping with us is smart, easy, fast, and sustainable. In 2024, the Group reported net sales of SEK 6.0 billion and has been listed on Nasdaq Stockholm since 2010.

https://news.cision.com/byggmax/r/interim-report-byggmax-group-1-january---31-march--2025%2Cc4136003

data.title: Polish utility PGE posts 2024 net loss, dragged by tax asset write-down
data.description:
PGE , Poland's largest power producer, reported a full-year 2024 net loss of 3.16 billion zlotys ($834.2 million), in line with preliminary results announced in March.

The results, announced on Tuesday evening, were affected by a deferred tax asset write-down of about 2.4 billion zlotys due to lower projected results from its coal-based activities.

PGE and other Polish utilities are under pressure from falling profitability from coal-fired power generation, having to cope with the cost of carbon emission rights and the growing share of electricity output being taken by renewables.

PGE has been transitioning to renewable energy, aligning with Poland's commitments under European Union climate policies. The company aims to achieve climate neutrality by 2050 and plans to reduce its carbon footprint.

"Taking into account the challenges facing the energy sector, in 2025, the PGE Group will intensify actions aimed at diversifying energy generation sources and strengthening the resilience of the national critical infrastructure," CEO Dariusz Marzec said in a statement.

"Our goal is to reduce future energy prices for end consumers by investing in modern generation capacities, energy storage, and smart grids.

Poland is one of the most coal-reliant countries in the European Union, given that, as of 2023, 70% of its electricity generation came from coal.

($1 = 3.7881 zlotys)

data.title: China Baoan Group 2024 Profit Falls 77%, Revenue Declines 34%
data.description:
China Baoan Group posted 2024 net profit attributable to shareholders of 172.6 million yuan, down 77% from 756.5 million yuan the previous year.

Earnings per share fell to 0.0669 yuan from 0.2933 yuan, according to a Wednesday filing with the Shenzhen bourse.

The equity investment and industrial management company's revenue declined 34% year over year to 20.2 billion yuan from 30.7 billion yuan.

China Baoan Group declared a dividend of 0.03 yuan per share.

Shares of the company declined 1% in recent trade.

data.title: ECB Expected to Maintain Reactive Rather Than Proactive Stance, Pimco SaysMarket Talk
data.description: The present environment leaves little room for the European Central Bank for forward guidance, says Pimco portfolio manager Konstantin Veit in a note. "We expect the Governing Council to maintain a reactive rather than proactive stance, and continue to stress that decisions will remain meeting-by-meeting​," he says. In line with the money market pricing, Pimco expects the ECB to cut the deposit rate by 25 basis points on Thursday to 2.25%.​ The ECB's June meeting will feature updated macroeconomic projections and it seems like a good opportunity for the ECB to "holistically" reassess whether an outright stimulative policy stance will be needed to defend its 2% price stability target, Veit says. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

data.title: Genscript Biotech Associate Logs $369 Million in Q1 Sales of CARVYKTI
data.description:
Genscript Biotech associate Legend Biotech generated about $369 million in net trade sales for its CARVYKTI cancer drug in the first quarter of 2025, according to a Tuesday filing.

CARVYKTI, developed by Nasdaq-listed Legend Biotech in partnership with Johnson & Johnson's Janssen Biotech, is an immunotherapy for the treatment of multiple myeloma.

Shares of Genscript Bio slipped almost 2% in late afternoon trade on Wednesday.

data.title: Alfakhera for Mens Tailoring Names New CEO
data.description:
Alfakhera for Mens Tailoring (SASE:9618) appointed Abdullah Suleiman Al-Yahya as its new chief executive officer, effective Thursday.

Al-Yahya will succeed Abdul Rahman Saeed Al Maleh, who will leave the position to avoid exceeding his probationary period, according to a Wednesday release by the tailoring company.

data.title: Playtech to Name John Gleasure as Chairman, Sky News Says, Citing Sources
data.description:
• Playtech will name former DAZN executive John Gleasure as its new chairman on Wednesday, succeeding Brian Mattingley, Sky News reports, citing unnamed sources.
  • Gleasure will replace Mattingley after the London-listed gambling-software company's annual meeting next month, Sky News says.
  • A Playtech spokesperson declined to comment.

Full story: https://tinyurl.com/5evkjrs4

Write to Najat Kantouar at najat.kantouar@wsj.com

data.title: ISOTeam Target Price Set at S$0.096 by CGS International>5WF.SG
ISOTeam Started at Add by CGS International >5WF.SG
New Zealand Shares Rebound
data.description:
New Zealand's benchmark S&P/NZX 50 index rose 0.5% to close at 12,068 on Wednesday, recovering from the previous session’s losses, buoyed by strength in the financial sector.

Heartland Group led the advance, jumping 7.1%.

Local shares of Australia banks Westpac and ANZ Group added 1.7% and 2.7%, respectively.

Among large-cap stocks, notable gains were also seen from Infratil (3.9%), Contact Energy (2.5%), and Mainfreight (1.1%).

Meanwhile, shares of Air New Zealand ended flat after the carrier projected lower earnings for 2025, amid uncertainty surrounding its global engine maintenance delays and related compensation levels.

Investors are now turning their attention to the first-quarter CPI data, scheduled for release on Thursday, for further cues on the Reserve Bank of New Zealand's monetary policy outlook.

Elsewhere, China, New Zealand’s largest trading partner, reported strong economic data but a trade war with the U.S. has cast a shadow over the outlook.

data.title: Amanat Holdings to Halt Trading in Dubai
data.description:

data.title: Deyaar Development to Halt Trading in Dubai
data.description:
Deyaar Development (DFM:DEYAAR) shares will temporarily pause trading on the Dubai Financial Market from 11 am local time on Wednesday due to its general assembly.

The Dubai-listed real estate developer will resume trading, pending the publication of the general assembly resolutions, according to a Wednesday release.

data.title: European Markets Head for Lower Open
data.description:
European equity markets were poised for a weaker start on Wednesday, as investor sentiment remained cautious in the face of U.S. President Donald Trump’s rapidly evolving trade policies.

In early premarket trade, Euro Stoxx 50 and Stoxx 600 futures fell 1.2% and 1%, respectively, pointing to a broad-based pullback across the region.

Traders also awaited key economic data releases, including UK inflation figures and Eurozone current account data, for further direction.

On the corporate front, sentiment was dented by ASML’s latest earnings report.

The Dutch semiconductor giant posted weaker-than-expected net bookings in the first quarter, raising concerns over a potential softening in chip demand.

data.title: Barclays Close to Selling Stake in Payments Business to Brookfield, Bloomberg Says, Citing Sources
data.description:
• Barclays is close to selling a stake in its payments business to Brookfield Asset Management, according to Bloomberg, citing unnamed sources.
  • Brookfield could initially buy a minority holding with an option to raise its stake within a three-to-seven-year window, Bloomberg reports.
  • An announcement could come as soon as this week with deliberations at an advanced stage, although no final agreements have been reached, the report says.
  • Barclays said it is exploring a number of investment options and that updates would be provided in due course, while Brookfield declined to comment, Bloomberg says.

Full story: https://shorturl.at/umdJq

Write to Nina Kienle at nina.kienle@wsj.com

data.title: Hong Kong Postal Service Halts U.S.-Bound Parcels
data.description:
By Tracy Qu

Hong Kong's postal service is halting shipments bound for the U.S. because it says the U.S. is "unreasonable, bullying and imposing tariffs abusively."

Hongkong Post also cited President Trump's executive order, effective May 2, eliminating duty-free "de minimis" treatment for low-value parcels from China and Hong Kong. The measure had allowed businesses such as Shein and Temu to avoid import taxes on shipments with a retail value of $800 or less.

Hong Kong's Hang Seng index was down more than 2% in Wednesday afternoon trading, more than other stock markets in the region.

The postal service said non-air transport of parcels bound for the U.S. has been halted immediately and airmail services will cease from April 27, while mail containing only documents will not be affected.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: Blencowe Resources Raises Fund via Share Placing
data.description:
Blencowe Resources , through its broker Tavira Financial, raised 1 million pounds sterling in gross proceeds through a placement of 33,333,334 shares at 0.03 pound apiece.

The mineral exploration and development company will utilize the net proceeds to finalize a 6,750-meter drilling program, advance the final stages of a definitive feasibility study for the Orom-Cross graphite project in Uganda, and for general working capital, according to a Tuesday filing.

Blencowe Resources will issue one warrant for each placing share, valid for two years and exercisable at 0.045 pound apiece. The company will raise an additional 1.5 million pounds if the warrants are exercised in full.

In a separate same-day filing, the company announced a retail offer to raise up to 100,000 pounds via a bookbuild at 0.03 pound apiece. The offer, which opened on Tuesday, is scheduled to close on Wednesday but may close early if oversubscribed.
